Patch Set 10: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Found one issue, but it only affects the case of very few pages in cache. Would be good to fix.

Is there some way this could be tested?  Is there a /proc or /sys file that triggers slab shrinking?  Something like "echo 3 > /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches", but that also tries to shrink the page cache which may result in per-page slab objects being freed rather than shrinking it via the slab cache. That said, if it is shrinking the slab cache before the page cache at least it would be exercising this code path a bit.

parents.subject: LU-7005 tests: wait client imports fully recovered

commit.subject: LU-6842 clio: add cl_page LRU shrinker
commit.message:
LU-6842 clio: add cl_page LRU shrinker

Register cache shrinker to reclaim memory from cl_page LRU list.

Signed-off-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam.xu@intel.com>
Change-Id: Id22fd1f1f8554dc03ac7313a58abd8cd3472ece0
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/15630
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
